{
  "decision": "fail",
  "risk": "medium",
  "work_stage": "rough-complete",
  "component_conditions": [
    "installed-defective",
    "non-compliant"
  ],
  "percent_complete": 90,
  "value": 0,
  "sound_source": "",
  "confidence": 1.0,
  "event_time": 0.0,
  "rate": 0.0,
  "order": [],
  "workable": [],
  "findings": [
    "Drywall panels installed tight to the floor with no gap",
    "Lack of expansion/movement gap at the bottom of the gypsum board"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Cut or trim the bottom edge of the drywall panels to establish a minimum 1/4-inch to 1/2-inch gap above the subfloor/floor line",
    "Verify that no fasteners are driven into the bottom track or plate within the gap zone"
  ],
  "rationale": "According to GA-216 (Specifications for the Application and Finishing of Gypsum Panel Products), gypsum board must be installed with a gap (typically 1/4 to 1/2 inch) at the floor line to prevent moisture wicking from the floor and to allow for structural movement/settling. Installing panels tight to the floor can cause buckling, cracking, or water damage.",
  "references": [
    "GA-216 Section 4.6",
    "IRC Section R702.3.1"
  ]
}
